tracking.controllers: Pimax Sword (optional Lighthouse faceplate for Index knuckles)

knownIssues.title: 3840×3840 per eye is the highest resolution in consumer VR — GPU-limited by design
knownIssues.description: Combined ≈29 megapixels per frame. Even an RTX 5090 cannot brute-force 90 FPS natively in most VR titles. Foveated rendering is not optional.
knownIssues.workaround: Always run Dynamic Foveated Rendering (requires eye tracking calibration). Combine with DLSS/FSR upscaling in titles that support it.

knownIssues.title: Aspheric vs Pancake optics — visible difference in clarity
knownIssues.description: The Crystal Super ships with interchangeable optics modules. Aspheric lenses give slightly better peak clarity; pancake lenses have wider sweet spot. Users report different optical experiences depending on which module is installed.
knownIssues.workaround: Try both modules if available. Pancake is the modern default and handles off-axis viewing better. Aspheric is preferred for flight sims where central clarity is critical.

knownIssues.title: Optional Lighthouse faceplate swaps inside-out for Lighthouse tracking
knownIssues.description: Standard Crystal Super uses inside-out cameras. Pimax sells an optional Lighthouse faceplate ($200-300) that enables base-station tracking for use with Index knuckles and Tundra trackers.
knownIssues.workaround: If you already own Lighthouse base stations + Index controllers, the faceplate upgrade is worthwhile. Inside-out is adequate for casual play.

optimizationTips.tip: Dynamic Foveated Rendering is mandatory — calibrate eye tracking first
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: At this resolution, DFR is the only way to hit 90+ FPS on any GPU. Run the eye tracking calibration in Pimax Client before your first play session.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Pimax Client → Display → Foveated Rendering → Dynamic (strong). DFR reduces effective shading by 50-60% in periphery. Without it, expect <40 FPS in any GPU-bound title even on an RTX 4090.

optimizationTips.tip: Lower SteamVR render resolution to 60-70% — native res is already 8K combined
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: SteamVR's default supersampling is catastrophic on this panel. Manually set render resolution to 60-70% for a massive GPU reclaim with minimal perceptual cost.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR → Video → Per-app render resolution → 60-70%. At 70% you're still rendering ~2688×2688 per eye, which exceeds any other consumer HMD's native resolution.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Pimax's native OpenXR runtime instead of SteamVR where possible
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Pimax's OpenXR runtime is optimized for this panel and supports foveated rendering natively. Less overhead than routing through SteamVR.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Windows OpenXR selector → Pimax. Verify via Pimax Client that native OpenXR is active. MSFS, DCS, iRacing, and most UE5 titles benefit.
